builtin_modules: System, System.Collections, System.Collections.Generic, System.IO, System.Linq, System.Text, System.Threading, System.Threading.Tasks, System.Net, System.Net.Http, System.Reflection, System.Runtime, System.Diagnostics, System.ComponentModel, System.Security, Microsoft.CSharp, Microsoft.VisualBasic
language_rules: 'using Namespace' and 'using static Class' both count as usage

Package-to-namespace mapping (NuGet ID → root namespace):
  Newtonsoft.Json → Newtonsoft.Json
  Microsoft.Extensions.* → Microsoft.Extensions.*
  Microsoft.AspNetCore.* → Microsoft.AspNetCore.*
  Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore → Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore
  Serilog.* → Serilog
  NLog → NLog
  log4net → log4net
  AutoMapper → AutoMapper
  FluentValidation → FluentValidation
  MediatR → MediatR
  Polly → Polly
  BouncyCastle.Cryptography → Org.BouncyCastle
  System.Text.Json → System.Text.Json (built-in ≥.NET 5, check TFM)
  Azure.* → Azure.*
  AWSSDK.* → Amazon.*
Implicit usings (.NET 6+): System, System.Collections.Generic, System.Linq, System.Text, System.Threading.Tasks, System.IO, System.Net.Http are auto-included — do NOT flag them as missing

Packages that require NO 'using' statement — treat as used if referenced in .csproj:
  Analyzer / CodeFix packages (*.Analyzers, *.CodeFix, StyleCop.Analyzers, SonarAnalyzer.*)
  Source generator packages (*.Generator, Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions)
  MSBuild integration packages (Microsoft.Build.*, Nerdbank.GitVersioning, MinVer)
  Runtime packs and targeting packs (Microsoft.AspNetCore.App, Microsoft.NETCore.App)
  PrivateAssets="all" packages are build-time tools — never flag as unused

Entity Framework Core: EF migration commands use the package even without direct 'using' in app code:
  Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design → used if migrations/ directory exists
  Database provider packages (Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.*, Pomelo.*) → used via DbContextOptionsBuilder extension methods

ASP.NET Core extension methods: packages like Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.* are used via
  services.AddAuthentication() / app.UseAuthentication() with no 'using' for the package namespace
  Check Startup.cs / Program.cs for Add*/Use* patterns

Dynamic patterns to flag:
  Assembly.LoadFrom(variable)
  Activator.CreateInstance(Type.GetType(var))
  AppDomain.CurrentDomain.Load(var)
missing_dep_rule: NuGet package ID is case-insensitive; normalize to lowercase for comparison
